questions.question: How long can tenant setting changes take to propagate across the Fabric tenant after being saved?

options.A: Immediately — changes are instant
options.B: Up to 5 minutes
options.C: Up to 15 minutes
options.D: Up to 1 hour

questions.explanation: The module states: 'Changes can take up to 15 minutes to take effect across the tenant.'

questions.question: At what minimum capacity SKU size can users with a Free license view Power BI content without needing a Pro license?

options.A: F8

questions.explanation: The F64 threshold means that on F64 or larger capacities, users with Free licenses can view Power BI reports and dashboards if they have a viewer role on the workspace. On capacities smaller than F64, a Pro or PPU license is required for viewing.

questions.question: What is the main difference between Workspace Apps and granting Workspace Access through roles?

options.A: Workspace Apps grant editing access; workspace roles grant read-only access
options.B: Workspace Apps provide read-only access to finished content for large audiences; workspace roles give direct workspace access for collaboration
options.C: Workspace Apps are for external users only; workspace roles are for internal users
options.D: Workspace Apps require a Pro license; workspace roles work with any license

questions.explanation: Workspace apps are recommended for large audiences — app consumers get read-only access to reports without seeing workspace contents or work in progress. Workspace roles (Admin, Member, Contributor, Viewer) give direct access for collaboration and development teams.

questions.question: What does the Microsoft Fabric Capacity Metrics app measure?

options.A: User login frequency across the tenant
options.B: Capacity unit (CU) consumption to monitor workload and capacity health
options.C: The number of Fabric items created per workspace
options.D: License utilization across the Microsoft 365 tenant

questions.explanation: Consumption in Fabric is measured in capacity units (CUs). The Microsoft Fabric Capacity Metrics app lets admins visualize CU consumption across their capacity to decide when to scale up or scale out.

questions.question: What is the difference between a 'Promoted' and 'Certified' endorsement for Fabric items?

options.A: Promoted and Certified are identical labels for the same endorsement process
options.B: Promoted can be set by workspace Contributors or Admins; Certified requires formal review by authorized certifiers configured via the Certification tenant setting
options.C: Promoted requires Fabric admin approval; Certified can be set by any workspace member
options.D: Promoted applies to semantic models only; Certified applies to all item types

questions.explanation: Promoted items are marked by workspace Contributor or Admin role users. Certified items require a formal review by an authorized certifier — the Fabric admin controls who can certify via the Certification tenant setting.

questions.question: What does it mean when the module says tenant settings are 'governance policies, not security measures'?

options.A: Tenant settings are only visible to Fabric admins and have no user-facing effect
options.B: Disabling a feature in tenant settings removes it from the UI, but doesn't prevent data access via other tools like Excel or Python APIs
options.C: Tenant settings apply only to external users accessing the tenant
options.D: Tenant settings cannot be changed after initial Fabric configuration

questions.explanation: The module states: 'Turning off the Export data setting removes the export button from the interface, but it doesn't prevent a user with read access to a semantic model from querying that model through another tool like Excel or Python. Real data security requires item permissions and sensitivity labels.'

questions.question: What is the difference between 'scaling up' and 'scaling out' a Fabric capacity?

options.A: Scaling up increases the F SKU size for more compute; scaling out moves some workspaces to a separate capacity to distribute load
options.B: Scaling up adds more workspaces to the capacity; scaling out increases the F SKU size
options.C: Scaling up doubles the number of domains; scaling out adds more workspace admins
options.D: Scaling up and scaling out are interchangeable terms for increasing capacity resources

questions.explanation: The module describes two options: scale up (increase the F SKU size to get more compute for the same capacity) versus scale out (move some workspaces to a separate capacity to distribute the load).

questions.question: Alex is the Fabric admin at Contoso and has purchased an F64 capacity. She wants all 200 users to be able to start creating Fabric items. What is the first tenant setting she must enable?

options.A: Export to Excel
options.B: Users can create Fabric items
options.C: Certification
options.D: Default sensitivity label

questions.explanation: The module describes this as 'the main Fabric on/off switch.' Without enabling 'Users can create Fabric items,' users cannot create lakehouses, warehouses, or any other Fabric items.

questions.question: Finance users at Contoso must not be able to export data to Excel, but all other departments should retain this capability. How should Alex configure the 'Export to Excel' tenant setting?

options.A: Disable Export to Excel for the entire organization
options.B: Enable Export to Excel for everyone except the 'Finance Restricted' security group
options.C: Enable Export to Excel only for the IT security group
options.D: Create a separate Fabric tenant for Finance users with the setting disabled

questions.explanation: Tenant settings can be enabled for everyone except specific security groups. Alex creates a 'Finance Restricted' group including Finance users, then enables Export to Excel for the entire organization except that group.

questions.question: Alex wants the Finance lead to manage their own certified content reviewers independently, without needing Alex to update the tenant-level Certification setting. What should she configure?

options.A: Create a separate Fabric tenant for Finance with its own Certification setting
options.B: Delegate the Certification tenant setting to the Finance domain admin, allowing them to override it for their domain
options.C: Assign Finance users the Fabric admin role so they can configure their own settings
options.D: Remove the Certification setting entirely from the tenant

questions.explanation: Delegated settings allow domain admins to override specific tenant-level settings for their domain. Alex delegates the Certification setting to the Finance domain admin, so the Finance lead can specify their own certifiers without Alex's involvement.

questions.question: Alex wants new Finance workspaces to automatically be assigned to the Finance domain as they are created, without manual intervention. What domain assignment method should she use?

options.A: Assign workspaces by name pattern matching on the word 'Finance'
options.B: Assign workspaces by workspace admin — workspaces whose admin is the Finance lead are automatically assigned to the Finance domain
options.C: Manually assign each new workspace in the Capacity settings tab
options.D: Enable auto-domain assignment in the Fabric admin portal settings page

questions.explanation: Alex chooses the workspace admin approach because it scales: 'As new Finance workspaces are created, Fabric automatically assigns them to the Finance domain if the Finance lead is the workspace admin.'

questions.question: Contoso's Finance team runs heavy quarter-end reports that push the F64 capacity toward its limit. What short-term action should Alex take, and which tool helps identify this pattern?

options.A: Delete Finance workspaces temporarily; identify using audit logs
options.B: Scale up the capacity to F128 for that week using the Capacity Metrics app (which shows CU consumption trends), then scale back afterward
options.C: Pause the Finance capacity during business hours to reset resources
options.D: Move Finance workspaces to shared capacity during quarter-end

questions.explanation: The module describes Alex proactively scaling the capacity to F128 for the week when Finance runs heavy closing reports, then scaling back. The Capacity Metrics app shows CU consumption trends that help identify when this is needed.

questions.question: A developer team's experimental pipeline runs on the F64 production capacity are interfering with Finance's daily reports. What should Alex do?

options.A: Grant Finance users higher workspace roles to override developer interference
options.B: Move development workspaces to a separate trial or test capacity to isolate them from production workloads
options.C: Delete the developer workspaces from the tenant
options.D: Disable development pipelines in the Fabric admin portal

questions.explanation: The module states that development work 'can consume significant compute and affect performance for production users. By keeping dev workspaces on a separate trial or test capacity, development activity doesn't compete with the Finance team's daily reports.'

questions.question: Alex needs to assign Power BI Pro licenses to 30 report-creating analysts. She wants the license to update automatically when people join or leave the analyst group. What approach should she use?

options.A: Manually assign a Pro license to each analyst individually in the Fabric portal
options.B: Create a Microsoft Entra ID security group (e.g., 'Contoso-Fabric-Pro-Users') and assign Pro licenses to the group in the M365 admin center under Billing > Licenses
options.C: Grant all analysts the Fabric admin role, which includes a Pro license
options.D: Purchase PPU licenses for each analyst as a one-time individual assignment

questions.explanation: License assignment is typically group-based. Alex manages 200 users by managing a handful of groups: 'When people join or leave a group, their license is updated automatically. This approach scales efficiently.'

questions.question: Contoso has 150 Marketing analysts and executives who only consume Power BI reports on the F64 capacity. Does Alex need to purchase Pro licenses for all 150 users?

options.A: Yes — Pro licenses are required for all users who view any Power BI content
options.B: No — because Contoso has an F64 capacity (meeting the F64 threshold), Free-licensed users can view Power BI reports; only the 30 report creators need Pro
options.C: Yes — but only for the executives, not the analysts
options.D: No — PPU licenses are sufficient for all 150 report consumers

questions.explanation: The F64 threshold means Free users can view Power BI reports on F64+ capacities. Alex 'realizes she doesn't need Pro licenses for the 150 Marketing analysts and Executive viewers... She only needs Pro for the 30 data analysts who create and publish Power BI reports.'

questions.question: Contoso's Marketing team has 120 users who need to view finished production-ready reports without seeing work-in-progress. What content distribution pattern should Alex recommend?

options.A: Grant all 120 users Contributor workspace role for full transparency
options.B: Publish a workspace app that provides read-only access to finished reports; consumers access via the app without workspace access
options.C: Share individual report items with each of the 120 users via item permissions
options.D: Grant all 120 users Admin workspace role so they can manage their own access

questions.explanation: Workspace apps are the recommended pattern for large audiences: 'When a workspace has an app, workspace admins publish the app to users who don't need workspace access. App consumers get read-only access to reports and dashboards without seeing the underlying workspace contents or work in progress.'

questions.question: Risk compliance asks Alex to confirm that no one exported sensitive Risk domain data to Excel last month. How should Alex verify this?

options.A: Run a KQL query in the workspace monitoring Eventhouse for export events
options.B: Check the audit logs in the Fabric admin portal, filtering by activity type 'Export to Excel' and location for Risk domain workspaces
options.C: Review the monitoring hub's historical runs filtered by Risk workspace
options.D: Open the Capacity Metrics app and filter by Risk domain activity

questions.explanation: The module describes this exact scenario: 'She searches the audit logs in the admin portal, filtering by activity type (Export to Excel) and user. She confirms no exports occurred from the Risk domain workspaces and provides the compliance team with the log evidence.'

questions.question: Alex opens the OneLake catalog Govern tab and sees Finance workspaces have only 30% sensitivity label coverage. What action should she take?

options.A: Delete all unlabeled Finance items immediately
options.B: Delegate this as an action item to the Finance domain admin to work with the data team to label remaining items
options.C: Apply a default sensitivity label manually to all Finance items from the admin portal
options.D: Disable the Finance domain workspaces until they are labeled

questions.explanation: The module describes Alex finding the 30% label coverage issue and delegating it: 'She delegates this as an action item to the Finance domain admin, who works with the data team to label the remaining items.'

questions.question: An analyst wants to find trusted, certified semantic models from the Finance domain for a campaign analysis. Where should they look, and what indicates a model is certified?

options.A: The monitoring hub; certified models show a green checkmark icon
options.B: The OneLake catalog, filtering by Finance domain; certified items show a Certified badge
options.C: The Fabric admin portal governance section; certified items appear in a separate list
options.D: The Microsoft 365 admin center; certified items appear under licensed content

questions.explanation: The module describes Finance users filtering the OneLake catalog by Finance domain to find certified semantic models, which appear with a Certified badge — indicating they went through a formal review by an authorized certifier.

questions.question: Alex wants to understand which Fabric features are being adopted across Contoso's departments one month after go-live. Which tool should she use?

options.A: The monitoring hub's historical activity runs
options.B: The admin monitoring workspace's Feature Usage and Adoption report
options.C: The Microsoft Fabric Capacity Metrics app
options.D: The audit logs in the Fabric admin portal

questions.explanation: The Feature Usage and Adoption report in the admin monitoring workspace shows which Fabric features users are using across the organization, usage trends over time, and which users and departments are most active.

questions.question: The Finance team reports that their daily report refresh is failing. Alex needs to identify which item failed, in which workspace, and what error occurred. What is the most efficient approach?

options.A: Review the Git commit history for the Finance workspace
options.B: Open the monitoring hub, filter by Status = Failed and Location = Finance workspace, then select the failed item to view error details
options.C: Check the Capacity Metrics app for Finance domain CU consumption spikes
options.D: Review the Feature Usage and Adoption report for the Finance workspace activity

questions.explanation: The module describes this scenario: 'Alex opens the monitoring hub, filters by Status = Failed and Location = Finance workspace, and finds two failed Dataflow Gen2 refreshes. She sees the error details and contacts the Finance data lead with the specific information.'

questions.question: Alex plans to pause a non-production dev/test capacity overnight to save costs. What important consideration does the module highlight?

options.A: Pausing a capacity automatically migrates workspaces to the production capacity
options.B: Pausing makes all content on that capacity unavailable — communicate planned downtime to users before pausing
options.C: Pausing a capacity permanently deletes all items in the associated workspaces
options.D: Pausing a capacity only stops semantic model refreshes; other activities continue normally

questions.explanation: The module states: 'Pausing a capacity makes all content on that capacity unavailable, so Alex only pauses non-production capacities and communicates planned downtime to users first.'

questions.question: A workspace admin asks Alex to create a new domain for their team's workspaces. Who is responsible for creating domains in Fabric?

options.A: Any workspace admin can create domains from their workspace settings
options.B: The Fabric admin creates domains from the admin portal Domains tab; domain admins manage workspaces within existing domains
options.C: Domain creation requires Microsoft Entra ID Global Administrator rights
options.D: Domains are automatically created when workspaces are assigned to a capacity

questions.explanation: Alex creates domains from the admin portal Domains tab. Domain admins (like the Finance and Risk leads) manage workspaces within their assigned domains and can designate domain contributors.

questions.question: Alex needs to delegate Finance administrative tasks to the Finance lead without giving them full tenant admin rights. What role should she assign?

options.A: Fabric admin — full tenant control
options.B: Domain admin for the Finance domain — manages workspaces within Finance and can override delegated tenant settings
options.C: Capacity admin for the F64 capacity — manages all workspaces on the capacity
options.D: Workspace admin for every individual Finance workspace

questions.explanation: Domain admin scope is limited to a specific domain. The Finance lead as domain admin can manage Finance workspaces and override delegated tenant settings — without getting tenant-wide control.

questions.question: A Power BI PPU user tries to create a lakehouse in a workspace not backed by an F capacity. What happens?

options.A: They can create the lakehouse because PPU includes all Fabric capabilities
options.B: They cannot create the lakehouse — PPU doesn't provision a Fabric capacity, and creating non-Power BI Fabric items requires an F capacity backing the workspace
options.C: They can create the lakehouse, but it will be stored in their personal My workspace only
options.D: They need to upgrade to a Power BI Pro license to create lakehouses

questions.explanation: The module states: 'PPU doesn't provision a Fabric capacity. PPU users can't create non-Power BI Fabric items like lakehouses, warehouses, or notebooks without an F capacity backing their workspace.'

questions.question: An audit reveals a workspace admin shared a report externally without authorization last week. How does Alex confirm this, and which tool provides the evidence?

options.A: The monitoring hub's job execution history for the workspace
options.B: The audit logs in the admin portal — which capture who shared content externally, when, and from where
options.C: The Capacity Metrics app's access log section
options.D: The Git commit history for items in the workspace

questions.explanation: Audit logs record user activities including who shared content externally, who modified workspace permissions, and who deleted Fabric items. Alex uses audit logs to track compliance and security events.

questions.question:
Sonia is the new Fabric admin at Fabrikam, a 500-person financial services company with an F64 Fabric capacity. She must configure the environment for three departments: Finance (10 engineers + 40 analysts), IT (20 engineers), and Marketing (200 report consumers + 30 report creators). Requirements:
(1) Finance data must not be exported to Excel
(2) Only designated data stewards can certify content
(3) Finance and IT need separate governance boundaries with their own admins
(4) Engineers creating Fabric items need Free licenses; report creators need Pro; 200 Marketing consumers need minimal-cost licenses
(5) Marketing report consumers should see finished content without workspace access
(6) IT dev workspaces must not impact Finance production capacity

Which configuration plan best addresses all six requirements?

options.A: Assign all 500 users Pro licenses; enable all tenant settings for everyone; create one shared domain; assign all workspaces to F64
options.B: (1) Enable 'Export to Excel' for all except 'Finance Restricted' group; (2) Enable Certification limited to 'Data Stewards' security group; (3) Create Finance domain (Finance lead as admin) and IT domain (IT lead as admin); (4) Pro for 30 Marketing creators; Free auto-assigned for engineers and 200 consumers (F64 threshold enables free viewing); (5) Publish workspace apps for 200 Marketing consumers; (6) Assign IT dev workspaces to a separate trial/test capacity
options.C: Create separate Fabric tenants for each department; assign all users Pro licenses; no domain configuration needed
options.D: Assign Admin workspace roles to all users; disable all exports tenant-wide; use only audit logs for governance

questions.explanation: (1) Tenant setting with security group exclusion blocks Finance exports only. (2) Certification setting limited to Data Stewards group controls who can certify. (3) Domains with admin delegation separate Finance and IT governance. (4) F64 threshold allows Free users to view Power BI reports; Pro only for 30 creators saves significant license costs. (5) Workspace apps give read-only access without workspace membership. (6) Separate capacity isolates dev workloads from production. Option B addresses all six requirements using the specific admin concepts described in the module.

questions.question: Alex enables the 'Users can create Fabric items' setting. A user reports they still cannot create a lakehouse 5 minutes after the change. What is the most likely explanation?

options.A: The user doesn't have a workspace available to create items in
options.B: Tenant setting changes can take up to 15 minutes to propagate; the user should wait and try again
options.C: The user needs the Fabric admin role to create items
options.D: The setting was applied to the wrong capacity

questions.explanation: Tenant setting changes can take up to 15 minutes to take effect across the tenant. Waiting and retrying is the appropriate first step.

questions.question: A tenant setting shows: 'Enabled for specific security groups: [Data-Engineers-Group].' Marketing analysts cannot access this feature. What change lets Marketing access the feature while keeping the current group's access?

options.A: Change the setting to 'Disabled for the entire organization'
options.B: Change the setting to 'Enabled for the entire organization' or add 'Marketing-Analysts-Group' to the specific groups list
options.C: Remove Data-Engineers-Group from the setting first, then re-add it
options.D: Create a new separate tenant setting specifically for the Marketing group

questions.explanation: Tenant settings support multiple states: disabled for all, enabled for all, enabled for specific groups, or enabled for all except specific groups. To include Marketing, either enable for all or add Marketing-Analysts-Group to the allowed groups list.

questions.question: A Power BI report is published to a workspace on an F32 capacity. A Free-licensed user tries to view the report and gets an access error. What is the cause?

options.A: The user needs to be added to a workspace role; change the workspace role to Viewer
options.B: The F32 capacity is below the F64 threshold — Free users cannot view Power BI content on sub-F64 capacities; the user needs a Pro or PPU license
options.C: The report needs to be republished on the F64 capacity to fix the error
options.D: The user needs a Fabric Capacity license to view Power BI content

questions.explanation: Free license users can only view Power BI content on capacities at the F64 SKU or larger. On F32 and smaller capacities, viewing Power BI content requires a Pro or PPU per-user license.

questions.question: A Finance domain is configured to auto-assign workspaces by workspace admin. A new workspace is created by an IT employee for a Finance project, but the Finance domain admin cannot see it in the Finance domain. What most likely caused this?

options.A: The workspace was assigned to the wrong capacity
options.B: The workspace admin is the IT employee, not the Finance lead — auto-assignment by workspace admin only includes workspaces where the Finance lead is the admin
options.C: Domains can only contain workspaces created directly by the domain admin
options.D: The Finance domain has reached its maximum workspace limit

questions.explanation: When assigning workspaces by workspace admin, Fabric automatically assigns workspaces to the domain based on who is the workspace admin. If the IT employee is the admin (not the Finance lead), the workspace is not auto-assigned to the Finance domain.

questions.question: Alex reviews the Feature Usage and Adoption report and sees Marketing has very low Fabric usage despite having licenses. What does this indicate and what should she do?

options.A: The Marketing team's licenses should be immediately revoked to save costs
options.B: There may be a training gap or adoption barrier — share this insight with the Marketing lead to investigate
options.C: The Marketing domain admin should be replaced with a more active admin
options.D: Marketing workspaces should be moved to a higher-capacity SKU

questions.explanation: The module describes this: 'She uses the Feature Usage and Adoption report to see that only IT and Finance are actively using Fabric. Marketing has low usage despite having licenses. She shares this insight with the Marketing lead to understand if there's a training gap or adoption barrier.'

questions.question: The IT capacity admin notices CU consumption on the F64 is regularly hitting 90%+ on Monday mornings due to Finance batch jobs. What are the two options available to address this?

options.A: Delete Finance workspaces on Mondays; or reduce Finance users' workspace permissions
options.B: Scale up (increase the F SKU size for more compute) or scale out (move some workspaces to a separate capacity to distribute load)
options.C: Pause the capacity on Monday mornings; or disable Finance batch pipeline triggers
options.D: Add more domains to the tenant; or increase workspace role limits

questions.explanation: The module describes these two options: 'Decide when to scale up (increase the F SKU size to get more compute) versus scale out (move some workspaces to a separate capacity to distribute load).'

questions.question: A workspace member marks a semantic model as 'Promoted.' A different user wants to mark it as 'Certified' but gets an error. What permission is required to certify items?

options.A: Workspace Admin role
options.B: Designation as an authorized certifier through the Certification tenant setting configured by the Fabric admin
options.C: Power BI Pro license
options.D: Microsoft Entra ID Global Administrator role

questions.explanation: Certified items 'require a formal review by an authorized certifier.' The Fabric admin controls who can certify items via the Certification tenant setting. Only users authorized through this setting can apply the Certified endorsement.

questions.question: A domain admin for the Finance domain wants to override the Certification tenant setting to designate Finance-specific certifiers. They open Finance domain settings but don't see Certification under 'Delegated settings.' What has the Fabric admin forgotten to do?

options.A: Assign the Finance lead as a workspace admin instead of domain admin
options.B: Make the Certification tenant setting delegatable in the admin portal so domain admins can override it for their domain
options.C: Create a security group for Finance certifiers in Microsoft Entra ID first
options.D: Enable the Certification setting tenant-wide before delegating it

questions.explanation: Delegated settings only appear in domain settings after the Fabric admin has made them delegatable. The module states: 'Some tenant settings can be delegated so domain admins can override them for their domain.' The Fabric admin must first configure which settings are delegatable.
